title: Multi-Protocol Access
description: Access the same data through MongoDB wire protocol and JavaScript. Prove protocol convergence — a MongoDB app can connect to Oracle without changing a single line of driver code.
checkpoint: You just wrote to relational tables using the MongoDB wire protocol. A MongoDB application can connect to Oracle without changing a single line of driver code.

exercises.title: MongoDB API via mongosh
exercises.description: List collections, query documents, insert through MongoDB API, and verify via SQL.
exercises.explanation: Oracle's ORDS (Oracle REST Data Services) implements the MongoDB wire protocol natively. That means mongosh — the official MongoDB shell — connects to Oracle and issues find(), insertOne(), deleteOne() against the same tables and collections you've been querying with SQL. Duality views appear as MongoDB collections, so an insertOne() through mongosh actually writes to normalized relational tables via the duality view's bidirectional mapping. No ETL, no sync, no driver changes. Your existing MongoDB applications can point at Oracle and immediately get ACID transactions, SQL joins, and the cost-based optimizer — while the app thinks it's still talking to MongoDB.

steps.label: List all collections visible through MongoDB wire protocol
Query a duality view as a MongoDB collection
Insert through MongoDB API — writes to relational tables via duality view

exercises.validationQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM client_interactions ci WHERE ci.data.client_id.number() = 1001

exercises.title: JavaScript with node-oracledb
exercises.description: Query duality views, insert with JSON type binding, and use the SODA API — all from JavaScript.
exercises.explanation: The node-oracledb driver gives JavaScript applications three distinct access patterns against the same Oracle database. First, SQL queries against duality views return native JavaScript objects — the JSON column maps directly to JS objects without parsing. Second, JSON type binding (oracledb.DB_TYPE_JSON) lets you insert JavaScript objects directly into JSON collections without serialization — Oracle stores them as binary OSON. Third, the SODA (Simple Oracle Document Access) API provides a document-store interface: createCollection, insertOne, find().filter() — the same mental model as MongoDB but backed by Oracle's transactional engine. All three patterns hit the same data, the same indexes, the same optimizer. Pick the access pattern that fits your code; the database handles convergence.

steps.label: Query a duality view — returns native JavaScript objects
Insert a document via JSON type binding
Verify and clean up the test record

exercises.validationQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM client_portfolio_dv WHERE json_value(data, '$._id' RETURNING NUMBER) = 1001
